summary: OpenMS is licensed under the 3-Clause BSD license.
Contributors should adhere to the OpenMS Code of Conduct when interacting with other developers, users, or community members.
OpenMS provides a Contributor Onboarding Guide for beginners new to open source or the project.

When reporting a bug in OpenMS, users should provide the OpenMS version they are running. When reporting a bug in OpenMS, users should provide the platform they are running OpenMS on. When reporting a bug in OpenMS, users should describe ho
When reporting a bug in OpenMS, users should provide data to reproduce the bug, preferably as a GitHub gist or via platforms like Dropbox or Google Drive.
OpenMS maintainers assign severity labels to issues to indicate if a bug is a blocker for a new release.
OpenMS has a developer guide available at https://openms.readthedocs.io/en/latest/manual/develop.html.
Pull request contributors must adhere to OpenMS coding conventions.
Pull request contributors must have unit tests and functional tests for their code. Pull request contributors must have proper documentation for their code.
Pull request contributors must have Python bindings with nanobind binding files located in src/pyOpenMS/bindings/.
OpenMS provides wrapping instructions in src/pyOpenMS/CLAUDE.md.
A core developer will review changes to the main development branch (develop) in OpenMS.
OpenMS core developers can be indicated as preferred reviewers using links in a comment section.
Early contact with OpenMS core developers may provide additional guidance on developing novel tools or algorithms.
OpenMS provides a Developer Guide with detailed coding conventions, architectural guidelines, and technical documentation.
